message:
feat: enrich policy denial reports with surface and rule columns, and
extract testable robot event validation helpers

Two related improvements that enhance audit observability and improve
code testability in the robot events pipeline.

crates/frankenterm-core/src/reports.rs — richer policy denial table:
- Policy Denials markdown table now includes Surface and Rule columns
  in addition to Time, Actor, Action, Decision, and Reason
- New policy_surface_from_decision_context() helper: parses the JSON
  decision_context string to extract the "surface" field, returning
  "—" for missing/malformed context (defensive against partial data)
- Pipe characters in surface and rule values are escaped for markdown
  table safety
- Updated existing tests to populate rule_id and decision_context
  fields so they exercise the new columns
- New unit test: policy_surface_from_decision_context handles valid
  JSON with surface, JSON without surface, invalid JSON, and None

crates/frankenterm/src/main.rs — surface-aware decision context +
extracted validation helpers:
- build_event_mutation_decision_context() now selects PolicySurface
  based on the interface parameter: "ft.events" → Mux,
  "ft.robot.events" → Robot, anything else → default_for_actor
  fallback (previously always used default_for_actor, which mapped
  human actors to Unknown instead of Mux)
- New validate_robot_events_cursor(cursor): extracts the negative
  cursor validation into a standalone pure function returning
  Result<(), &'static str>
- New validate_robot_events_replay_limit(replay_limit): extracts the
  zero replay-limit validation similarly
- New next_robot_events_cursor(cursor, event_ids): extracts the
  next_cursor computation into a standalone function that preserves
  the checkpoint value when the replay page is empty (previously the
  inline code used cursor_mode bool, now uses cursor.is_some())
- run() wired to use the three new helpers, replacing inline checks
- 5 new unit tests: cursor validation rejects negative / accepts
  zero+None, replay_limit validation rejects zero / accepts 1+None,
  next_cursor preserves checkpoint on empty page, next_cursor
  advances to last event ID, decision context fallback for unknown
  interface
- Updated existing test assertions: ft.events interface now correctly
  produces PolicySurface::Mux instead of Unknown

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
